title: Why I rebuilt this site
excerpt: A short note on what changed, what's here, and what's coming.
body: The original single-page portfolio was fine for a bio, but it couldn't hold anything that wasn't a bullet point. This version makes room for the work itself — 214 bylines, 72 certifications, three academic papers, the services I quietly run on the side, and a blog for the things that don't fit anywhere. More soon.

title: Fieldnotes: Dhaka, April 2026
excerpt: Early signals from the pilot survey — a handful of things I didn't expect.
body: Three things from the first 40 responses. (1) Students report using Facebook less than I'd assumed, but trust what they see on it more than what they see on YouTube — interesting inversion. (2) Age 20–22 is the inflection point: respondents older than that show dramatically better lateral-reading habits. (3) Self-reported media literacy barely correlates with the behavioural tasks I'm running. Too early to say anything publishable, but the instrument is holding.

title: Teaching notes: fossilized errors
excerpt: What I'm trying with two batches of first-year Academic English students.
body: "Fossilized error" is a clunky term but a useful one — structural mistakes that have survived years of schooling because no one caught them in time. My current experiment: each student maintains an error log for the first four weeks, and we spend ten minutes per session on shared patterns. Early impression: the log is doing more than my feedback ever did.
